title: Pensions and Fertility: Microeconomic Evidence
notes: Direct old-age-security pension generosity shock with fertility outcomes.

title: The Old-Age Security Motive for Fertility: Evidence from the Extension of Social Pensions in Namibia
notes: Direct old-age-security pension expansion with fertility outcomes.

title: What Explains Fertility? Evidence from Italian Pension Reforms
notes: Direct test of pension generosity and fertility under old-age-security versus consumption motives.

title: The impact of long-term care insurance on family fertility behaviour: evidence from China
notes: Fertility outcome and social insurance treatment; mechanism includes elder-care risk rather than pure pension wealth.

title: The Introduction of Bismarck's Social Security System and its Effects on Marriage and Fertility in Prussia
notes: Historical social-insurance treatment with marriage and fertility outcomes. Companion corrigendum: literature/pdfs/old-age-security-pension-crowdout/guinnane-streb-2022-bismarck-social-security-marriage-fertility-prussia-corrigendum-population-and-development-review.pdf

title: The fertility effects of public pension: Evidence from the new rural pension scheme in China
notes: Direct rural pension expansion with fertility outcomes.

title: Children as insurance revisited: Impact of children on private insurance adoption among older parents
notes: Mechanism evidence: children and private insurance are studied as old-age risk substitutes; fertility is treatment rather than outcome.

title: Pensions and Fertility: Evidence from Germany
notes: Historical Bismarck pension exposure with fertility outcomes; strong FDT transportability.

title: Fertility and Financial Development: Evidence from U.S. Counties in the 19th Century
notes: Indirect old-age-security test through financial-market development and fertility outcomes.

title: The US baby boom and the 1935 Social Security Act
notes: Fertility outcome and Social Security Act treatment; mechanism includes family allowances and broader social spending rather than pure old-age pension insurance.
